content:
type: SURVEY
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
note: NDCG.compute: fix IDCG calc to iterate over len(expected_ids) instead of min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))

fields.note: NDCG.compute: fix IDCG calc to iterate over len(expected_ids) instead of min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))

content:
type: DEFER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
spec: In NDCG.compute(), fix the IDCG calculation: currently it computes ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids)) and sums discounted_gain(rel=True, i=i, mode=mode) for i in range(1, ideal_length+1). Change this so ideal_length = len(expected_ids) (i.e. IDCG must be computed strictly from the number of expected/relevant documents, NOT clamped by the number of retrieved documents). So: idcg = sum(discounted_gain(rel=True, i=i, mode=mode) for i in range(1, len(expected_ids) + 1)). Remove the min()-based clamping and its comment ('Calculate IDCG using min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))... Since we can't achieve better than perfect ranking of all relevant docs'). Keep everything else in NDCG.compute (DCG calc, edge case handling for idcg==0, return statement) unchanged aside from this. This aligns IDCG with the standard NDCG definition.
note: This is the only change I need in this file; happy for you to own it and layer your relevance_scores feature on top

Change this so ideal_length = len(expected_ids) (i.e. IDCG must be computed strictly from the number of expected/relevant documents, NOT clamped by the number of retrieved documents). So: idcg = sum(discounted_gain(rel=True, i=i, mode=mode) for i in range(1, len(expected_ids) + 1)). Remove the min()-based clamping and its comment ('Calculate IDCG using min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))... Since we can't achieve better than perfect ranking of all relevant docs'). Keep everything else in NDCG.compute (DCG calc, edge case handling for idcg==0, return statement) unchanged aside from this. This aligns IDCG with the standard NDCG definition.
fields.note: This is the only change I need in this file; happy for you to own it and layer your relevance_scores feature on top

content:
type: SPEC
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/evaluation/retrieval/metrics.py
spec: NDCG.compute() IDCG fix: replace 'ideal_length = min(len(retrieved_ids), len(expected_ids))' with 'ideal_length = len(expected_ids)'. The idcg sum stays 'sum(discounted_gain(rel=True, i=i, mode=mode) for i in range(1, ideal_length + 1))' but now driven purely by the count of expected relevant docs, not clamped by retrieved_ids length. Remove/update the now-inaccurate comment about min(). No other behavior changes: DCG calc over retrieved_ids unchanged, idcg==0 edge case still returns score=0.0, ndcg_score = dcg/idcg unchanged. Example: expected_ids has 5 items, retrieved_ids has 3 items (all 3 relevant) -> IDCG must be computed over all 5 ideal positions (not just 3), so ndcg_score will correctly be less than 1.0 reflecting that only 3 of 5 relevant docs could possibly have been retrieved in this top_k window.
note: Confirms the DEFER spec sent earlier

The idcg sum stays 'sum(discounted_gain(rel=True, i=i, mode=mode) for i in range(1, ideal_length + 1))' but now driven purely by the count of expected relevant docs, not clamped by retrieved_ids length. Remove/update the now-inaccurate comment about min(). No other behavior changes: DCG calc over retrieved_ids unchanged, idcg==0 edge case still returns score=0.0, ndcg_score = dcg/idcg unchanged. Example: expected_ids has 5 items, retrieved_ids has 3 items (all 3 relevant) -> IDCG must be computed over all 5 ideal positions (not just 3), so ndcg_score will correctly be less than 1.0 reflecting that only 3 of 5 relevant docs could possibly have been retrieved in this top_k window.
